misconceptions: Listing devices without linking to purpose/audience
Treating synthesis as summary of sources instead of an original position
Confusing tone with mood or topic
Assuming longer quotes equal stronger evidence
Ignoring line of reasoning / only stacking claims
paper_rules: MCQ: reading nonfiction passages; select best evidence or rhetorical effect
FRQ1 Synthesis: use provided sources to build an argument
FRQ2 Rhetorical analysis: explain how choices advance purpose for audience
FRQ3 Argument: defend, challenge, or qualify a claim with original reasoning
Score essays on thesis, evidence/commentary, sophistication where applicable
units_summary: U4 Audience Purpose and Context (w≈0.11, q=55)
U5 Style Choices and Effects (w≈0.11, q=55)
